intent: Propose one falsifiable non-monotonicity question and an exact benchmark decomposition for consumable quality certificates, without solving it or asserting a direction.

entity.title: Can cheaper consumable certificates generate an interior reversal in buyer outcomes?
entity.summary: A proposal to test whether the equilibrium certificate inventory and its trade-triggered depletion can make inspection, successful matching, or buyer surplus non-monotone in certification cost.

payload.phenomenon: As certification cost k varies, endogenous issuance, carryover, and trade-triggered depletion change the stationary stock of visible certificates and the capacity composition of sellers left uncertified; this may change the return to inspecting an uncertified seller.
payload.object_to_explain: Fix an admissible capacity process, seller success margin, discount factor, search cost c, and one public equilibrium-selection rule used at every k. In the selected stationary symmetric Markov equilibrium, define I(k) as the ex ante probability that the arriving buyer pays c to inspect, A(k) as the ex ante probability that trade is with a high-capacity seller, and U_B(k)=A(k)-c I(k) under the seed's unit service-value normalization. Determine whether there are 0<k1<k2<k3 and at least one Y in {I,A,U_B} such that [Y(k2)-Y(k1)][Y(k3)-Y(k2)]<0, and whether any such strict turning point is removed or altered by the declared stock, cost-accounting, and inspection benchmarks.
payload.unresolved_delta: It is unresolved whether any strict three-cost turning point is generated by certificate carryover and trade depletion rather than by direct certification-cost accounting, endogenous inspection adjustment, or a change in equilibrium selection.
payload.importance: The answer determines whether a reduction in the private cost of producing public information can be evaluated using only its effect on current certification, or instead requires tracking an endogenous inventory of signals, the residual uncertified pool, and buyers' acquisition of private information. This matters for interpreting certification subsidies and disclosure technologies without presuming their buyer-side ranking.
payload.kill_condition: Kill the proposed consumable-stock explanation if a characterization over the stated admissible primitives and a fixed selection rule rules out the strict three-cost sign-change test for all three outcomes, or if every apparent full-model turning point is reproduced with the same selected outcome path after certificate carryover and trade-triggered depletion are removed. A reversal created only by switching equilibrium selections, or one that survives solely through endogenous inspection in the stock-free benchmark, does not count as support for the proposed channel.
payload.proposed_scope: Two long-lived sellers, an infinite sequence of short-lived buyers, privately observed seller capacity, truthful certification available only with high capacity at cost k, a public certificate state that persists until a trade with its seller consumes it, buyer choice among inspection of one uncertified seller at cost c, purchase without inspection, and exit, and stationary symmetric Markov strategies evaluated under one fixed public selection rule. The task is framing and decomposition only, not equilibrium solution or welfare ranking.
prohibited_claims: No direction of any comparative static is asserted.
No existence, uniqueness, proof, validation, or welfare result is asserted.
No literature novelty or priority claim is asserted.
No benchmark difference is interpreted causally unless the declared policies, primitives, state law, and equilibrium selection are held as specified.

entity.title: Benchmarks for certificate cost, stock, depletion, and inspection
entity.summary: Five common-selection comparisons separate the full consumable-certificate environment from stock-free flow certification, nondepleting inventory, mechanical cost accounting, and a frozen inspection policy.

benchmarks.label: Full consumable-stock model with endogenous inspection
exact_primitives: Two long-lived sellers and one short-lived buyer per period.
Each seller privately observes its current high or low service capacity according to the common admissible capacity process.
A seller with high capacity and no visible certificate may pay k to create one truthful public certificate; an unconsumed certificate remains publicly visible.
The buyer observes the public certificate state, may pay c to inspect one uncertified seller, may buy without inspection, or may exit.
A trade with a certified seller consumes that seller's visible certificate; no trade leaves it in stock.
Seller success margin and discount factor, buyer service value one, capacity process, and equilibrium selection are held common across all comparisons.
timing: The inherited public certificate stock is observed.
Uncertified sellers privately observe current capacity and eligible high-capacity sellers choose whether to pay k for certification.
The buyer observes the updated public certificate state.
The buyer chooses whether to inspect one uncertified seller at cost c.
After any inspection result, the buyer chooses a seller, buys without further inspection, or exits.
Service success and payoffs are realized.
A certificate is removed exactly when its seller trades; every other visible certificate carries to the next period.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium, using one declared public selection rule at every k and reporting the correspondence rather than switching selections when uniqueness fails.
benchmarks.prediction: No direction is imposed. For each Y in {I,A,U_B}, test the strict three-cost sign-change criterion and record the selected stationary uncertified-pool composition and certificate-stock distribution at each k.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: This benchmark contains the direct certification-cost effect, endogenous certificate issuance, inventory carryover, trade-triggered depletion, endogenous pool composition, and endogenous inspection response jointly.

benchmarks.label: Stock-free one-period certificate flow
exact_primitives: All primitives, information, actions, payoffs, and the equilibrium-selection rule equal the full model.
Every certificate expires before the next period whether or not its seller trades, so each period begins with zero inherited certificate stock.
Certification and buyer inspection remain endogenous within the period.
timing: Each period begins with no inherited certificate.
Capacity observation, certification, buyer observation, optional inspection, purchase or exit, and service occur in the same order as in the full model.
All certificates are deleted at the end of the period before the next capacity observations.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under the same public selection rule used for the full model.
benchmarks.prediction: The full-minus-flow comparison is the diagnostic for interperiod certificate inventory, including the composition created by carrying unconsumed certificates forward; its sign is left open.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether removing all carryover eliminates or changes any strict turning point while preserving contemporaneous cheaper certification and endogenous inspection.

benchmarks.label: Persistent but nondepleting certificate stock
exact_primitives: All primitives, information, actions, payoffs, and the equilibrium-selection rule equal the full model.
A visible certificate carries forward after both trade and no trade; trade never consumes it.
Certification and inspection remain endogenous whenever the declared state permits those actions.
timing: The inherited public certificate stock is observed.
Capacity, certification, buyer observation, optional inspection, choice, and service follow the full-model order.
The post-trade transition retains every visible certificate instead of deleting the certificate of the traded seller.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under the same public selection rule used for the full model.
benchmarks.prediction: The full-minus-nondepleting comparison is the diagnostic for trade-triggered depletion conditional on permitting certificate carryover; its sign is left open.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether the event-contingent removal of a certificate, rather than persistence alone, changes the uncertified pool and the three-cost test.

benchmarks.label: Reference-policy mechanical certification-cost accounting
exact_primitives: Choose and disclose one reference cost k0 in the interior of the admissible cost interval.
Freeze the certification policy, certificate-stock transition kernel, buyer inspection and purchase-or-exit policies, and induced stationary distribution at their selected full-model values for k0.
Replace only the seller's payment k0 by k in payoff accounting; do not re-solve behavior or the state distribution.
timing: Generate public and private histories using the frozen k0 policies and transition law.
Apply the same trades, service outcomes, and certificate depletion events at every counterfactual k.
Change only the certification payment entered in seller payoff accounting.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Fixed-policy accounting counterfactual, explicitly not a new equilibrium for k different from k0.
benchmarks.prediction: By construction I(k), A(k), and U_B(k) are constant in this accounting comparison; only the seller's certification expenditure changes mechanically. Any buyer-outcome response therefore belongs outside the direct accounting term.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: How much of the re-equilibrated full-model response remains after removing this mechanical payment term.

benchmarks.label: Reference inspection policy with re-equilibrated certificate stock
exact_primitives: Choose the same disclosed reference cost k0 used in the mechanical benchmark.
At every public state and admissible information set, constrain the buyer's inspect-or-not decision to the selected full-model inspection rule at k0.
For each k, allow seller certification, certificate inventory, trade depletion, buyer seller choice after the constrained inspection decision, and the stationary distribution to re-equilibrate.
All remaining primitives and the public selection rule equal the full model.
timing: The public stock and private capacities evolve as in the full model.
Sellers choose certification at the current k.
The buyer applies the fixed state-contingent inspect-or-not rule from k0, observes any resulting signal, and then makes the permitted purchase-or-exit choice.
Trade consumes a certificate and the constrained equilibrium state law updates.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Constrained selected stationary symmetric Markov equilibrium with only the inspect-or-not map fixed at its k0 value.
benchmarks.prediction: The full-minus-inspection-frozen comparison is the diagnostic for adjustment of the endogenous inspection rule; the inspection-frozen path retains re-equilibrated certification, stock, composition, and depletion. No sign is imposed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether a full-model turning point survives when the buyer cannot adjust the inspection rule as k changes, and whether its stock-free counterpart does the same.

payload.exact_question_delta: Using the same admissible primitives and public equilibrium-selection rule, compare the vector (I,A,U_B) in the full model with: (i) zero certificate carryover, (ii) carryover without trade depletion, (iii) frozen-policy cost accounting, and (iv) a fixed state-contingent inspection rule. Attribute no direction in advance: the stock diagnostic is full versus flow, the depletion diagnostic is full versus nondepleting stock, the mechanical cost diagnostic is the fixed-policy accounting comparison, and the endogenous-inspection diagnostic is full versus inspection-frozen. A claimed consumable-stock explanation must be tied to a strict three-cost sign change and must not be an equilibrium-selection switch.

outcome.rationale: The candidate proposes one exact three-cost falsifiability test and a linked benchmark set that separates carryover, trade depletion, mechanical cost accounting, and endogenous inspection without claiming a comparative-static direction or result.
